# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a Jenkins Pipeline that builds Docker images with user-provided image tags and registry URLs. Accept these as build parameters and use them directly in docker build and push commands. This allows teams to deploy to different registries without modifying the pipeline.

Prevent cicd security.
# Category: cicd_security

import jenkins.model.*
 
def call() {   // Declare a method named 'call' that will be executed when Jenkins runs this Pipeline script   
println "Building Docker image with tag ${params['TAG']} and registry URL  ${params['REGISTRY_URL']}"     # Print the parameters to console for debugging purposes   // This line is optional, you can remove it if not needed.  It's just here as a reminder that Jenkins will print out all of your build steps   
println "Pushing Docker image with tag ${params['TAG']} and registry URL  ${params['REGISTRY_URL']}"     // Print the parameters to console for debugging purposes   // This line is optional, you can remove it if not needed.  It's just here as a reminder that Jenkins will print out all of your build steps
   
// Get instance of current Jenkins job (the one running this Pipeline script) and get the underlying workspace directory    
def jenkins = Jenkins.instance   // This line is optional, you can remove it if not needed  It's just here as a reminder that 'Jenkins.' will be used to access other parts of your application   
println "Workspace dir: ${jenkins.workspace}"     // Print the workspace directory for debugging purposes   This line is optional, you can remove it if not needed  It's just here as a reminder that 'Jenkins.' will be used to access other parts of your application
println "Building Docker image in ${jenkins.workspace}"     // Print the workspace directory for debugging purposes   This line is optional, you can remove it if not needed  It's just here as a reminder that 'Jenkins.' will be used to access other parts of your application
println "Pushing Docker image in ${jenkins.workspace}"     // Print the workspace directory for debugging purposes   This line is optional, you can remove it if not needed  It's just here as a reminder that 'Jenkins.' will be used to access other parts of your application
   
// Build Docker image with provided tag and registry URL using Jenkin's build step     // Replace the placeholders in this line. The actual command should look like: docker build -t my-image . --build-arg REGISTRY_URL=myregistryurl/username/. 
// Push Docker image with provided tag and registry URL using Jenkin's step     // Replace the placeholders in this line. The actual command should look like: docker push my-image@tag . --registry-url=${params['REGISTRY_URL']}   This is just a placeholder, you need to replace it with your own commands
    }  else {     // If Jenkins was not able to find the job (for example because of an incorrect name), then we should print out why.      Println "Job '${env.JOB_NAME}' does not exist"   This line is optional, you can remove it if not needed
}  }    else {     // If Jenkins was unable to get the current job (for example because of an incorrect name), then we should print out why      Println "Unable to determine currently executing Job. Please run this script from a build step."   This line is optional, you can remove it if not needed
}  }    // End 'call' method definition     The end brace for the call function marks where Jenkins will start looking at your Pipeline configuration file (Jenkinfile) to know how and when run this script should be. It must match exactly with what is written below in braces {} of above code
